description:
This is a false-colour satellite image example of Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) Land Use / Land Cover (LULC) Class 1.2.1.0 for the city of Skien, Norway. The image was acquired on 2012-08-11 by SPOT-5 satellite at 2.5m resolution, using a 1/2/3 Band Combination. The source is CNES 2011©, Distribution Airbus DS/Spot Image.

The false-colour composite shows dense vegetation and agricultural fields in shades of red, urban fabric and impervious surfaces in bluish-grey, and water bodies (a river) in dark blue/green. Overlaid in turquoise are prominent linear features representing transport infrastructure, including a large bridge crossing the river and associated road networks, explicitly labelled "1.2.1.0". This class corresponds to 'Road and rail networks and associated land' within the CLC+ nomenclature guideline. The highlighted infrastructure connects densely built-up urban areas on both banks of the river and extends into surrounding vegetated and cultivated landscapes.
